questions.stem: A pediatric patient develops this severe mucocutaneous reaction with extensive facial epidermal detachment and crusting shortly after starting a fluoroquinolone. Which drug reaction is shown?

explanation.summary: The widespread epidermal detachment with mucosal and periorificial involvement is characteristic of the SJS/TEN spectrum, a rare but life-threatening hypersensitivity reaction; affected patients must avoid the offending fluoroquinolone permanently.
explanation.whyCorrect: The widespread epidermal detachment with mucosal and periorificial involvement is characteristic of the SJS/TEN spectrum, a rare but life-threatening hypersensitivity reaction; affected patients must avoid the offending fluoroquinolone permanently.

questions.stem: These three antibacterial molecules share a common bicyclic core, but the two agents on the right add a substituent (highlighted at the top of the rightmost structure) that greatly broadens their clinical spectrum. To which antibiotic class do the modified agents belong?

explanation.summary: The core is the quinolone ring; adding a fluorine atom (seen on nalidixic acid's successors) yields the fluoroquinolones, which have broader gram-negative coverage and stronger binding to type II topoisomerases than the parent quinolone.
explanation.whyCorrect: The core is the quinolone ring; adding a fluorine atom (seen on nalidixic acid's successors) yields the fluoroquinolones, which have broader gram-negative coverage and stronger binding to type II topoisomerases than the parent quinolone.

questions.stem: This schematic depicts a type II topoisomerase acting on interlinked bacterial DNA, progressively reducing the number of braided crossings shown. What enzymatic function is being illustrated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Synthesis of the leading DNA strand

explanation.summary: The stepwise reduction from 4 to 0 braids shows topoisomerase IV passing one duplex through another to separate (decatenate) the two completed bacterial chromosomes; DNA gyrase performs the related task of managing negative supercoils.
explanation.whyCorrect: The stepwise reduction from 4 to 0 braids shows topoisomerase IV passing one duplex through another to separate (decatenate) the two completed bacterial chromosomes; DNA gyrase performs the related task of managing negative supercoils.

questions.stem: The listed drugs are shown binding an enzyme located on the replicating chromosome inside this bacterial cell. What is the immediate consequence of this drug-enzyme interaction?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Accumulation of double-strand DNA breaks that halt replication and kill the cell

choices.label: D
choices.text: Blockade of ribosomal peptide bond formation

choices.label: E
choices.text: Inhibition of cell wall peptidoglycan cross-linking

explanation.summary: Fluoroquinolones trap the enzyme after it has nicked DNA, preventing religation; the resulting persistent double-strand breaks stop DNA synthesis and produce a bactericidal effect.
explanation.whyCorrect: Fluoroquinolones trap the enzyme after it has nicked DNA, preventing religation; the resulting persistent double-strand breaks stop DNA synthesis and produce a bactericidal effect.
